title: Calculating due dates using AutoBoost formula

posts.author: maalev (Maayan Levy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
I’m pretty new in automations on Monday.
I’m trying to trigger an automation to set the Due Dates of all items in the board according to the number of business days it takes to complete them (a column I created and filled). Found out about AutoBoost, which can calculate exactly that. I can’t seem to figure out to use the formula tho, tried using ADD_WORKDAYS(TODAY(), 5) to begin with, and it didn’t work.

Does anyone know how to use it properly?
Thank you!

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @maalev,
Welcome to the community forum

It would be great if you can explain your problem in Detail.
However, I have set up solution for similar problem.
In that, I have to set up due dates**(Action)** on the basis on a “Task type” (Trigger).
In such a way that due date don’t arrive on Saturday and Sunday.
For this i set up an automation in such a way that skip Saturday and Sunday.

image1368×487 31 KB

Workday is custom Field (Formula) " WEEKDAY ( TODAY ( ) ,1"

Zaki
Business Workflow Consultant
Ready Logic
